vocabulary.definition: A person who writes a book, article, or other text.
vocabulary.example: The author of the novel won several awards.

vocabulary.definition: A fictional or specific place, city or region name.
vocabulary.example: The story is set in Caprona, a city full of magic.

vocabulary.definition: A main division of a book, typically numbered or titled.
The legal right to control the use and distribution of a creative work.
vocabulary.example: The book is protected by copyright laws.

vocabulary.definition: The outside part of a book or magazine that protects the pages.
vocabulary.example: The cover of the book was beautifully illustrated.

vocabulary.definition: A note at the beginning of a book dedicating it to someone.
vocabulary.example: The author wrote a touching dedication to her parents.

vocabulary.definition: People who use magic or perform magic tricks.
vocabulary.example: The magicians amazed the audience with their incredible tricks.

vocabulary.definition: A brief comment or explanation often included in a text.
vocabulary.example: The author's note explained the inspiration behind the story.

vocabulary.definition: A person or company that prepares and issues books or other works for sale.
vocabulary.example: The publisher released the new edition last month.

vocabulary.definition: A list of the chapters or sections in a book or document.
vocabulary.example: Please check the table of contents to find the chapter you need.

vocabulary.definition: The page at the beginning of a book that shows the title and author.
vocabulary.example: The title page gave the author's name and the book's title.
